verdict_reasoning: HSPD1 is a mitochondrial matrix chaperonin (Hsp60) canonically resident in the mitochondrial inner compartment. However, multiple lines of evidence establish a documented non-baseline surface pool: (1) cancer-cell ecto-display — tumor cells expose Hsp60 on the outer leaflet where it serves as a DAMP and immune activation ligand; (2) cell-death-induced surface translocation — apoptotic and pyroptotic cells externalize mitochondrial Hsp60 to the PM; (3) documented innate immune signaling function — the NCBI summary notes function as a signaling molecule in innate immunity, which in the literature occurs via cell-surface and extracellular Hsp60 acting as a DAMP ligand for TLR4 and RAGE. The canonical biology is mitochondrial-matrix chaperone function, but the protein reaches the outer leaflet under stress, cell-death, and oncogenic states via documented mechanisms.
